text: The Spectra package splits the backend based on a defined factor and processes each in parallel for parallel processing.
source_excerpt:
For parallel processing, `Spectra` splits the backend based on a defined
`factor` and processes each in parallel (or *in serial* if a `SerialParam` is
used).

text: The default implementation for backendParallelFactor() returns an empty factor, not suggesting any preferred splitting.
source_excerpt:
The default
implementation for `backendParallelFactor()` is to return an empty `factor`
(`factor()`) hence not suggesting any preferred
splitting.

text: The backendParallelFactor() for MsBackendMzR returns a factor based on the data files the data is stored in.
source_excerpt:
`backendParallelFactor()` for `MsBackendMzR` on the other hand
returns a `factor` based on the data files the data is stored in (i.e. based
on the `dataStorage` of the MS data).

text: Chunk-wise processing can reduce the memory demand for operations because only the peak data of the current chunk needs to be realized in memory.
source_excerpt:
Besides parallel processing, this chunk-wise processing can also reduce the
memory demand for operations, because only the peak data of the current chunk
needs to be realized in memory.
